description_long: This cowshed is a large, L-shaped agricultural building constructed primarily from light-colored wooden planks, with a prominent red gable roof covering both wings. The structure features multiple large, vertically slatted wooden doors, including a double-door at the end of one wing and two wide sliding barn doors along the longer side, providing ample access for livestock or equipment. The roof is steeply pitched and extends over both sections, with the intersection forming a distinct junction. The exterior walls are uniformly clad in horizontal timber, and there are no visible windows, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The doors are reinforced with black metal hardware, and the overall appearance is robust and functional, suitable for housing cattle or storing feed. The absence of visible ventilation features or decorative elements suggests a focus on practicality and durability.
description: A large, L-shaped wooden cowshed with a red gable roof, multiple wide barn doors, and a utilitarian, windowless design.
description_view_0: The front view shows the L-shaped layout with a double-door on the left wing and two large sliding barn doors on the longer side.
description_view_1: This view highlights the end of the shorter wing with the double-door and the long expanse of the red gable roof.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the uninterrupted length of the building's back wall, with no doors or windows visible.
description_view_3: This side view shows a single door on the shorter wing and the intersection of the two roof sections.
attribute_door_style: double wooden barn door with black metal hardware; wide sliding barn doors
